standards_checked.no-inline-code: pass — these are .py scripts, not command/agent .md files; standard does not apply
standards_checked.no-hardcoded-values: pass — files contain only static step-label strings and stdlib json import; no paths, URLs, or environment-specific values
standards_checked.use-source-venv: pass — no python3 invocations found in any file
standards_checked.integer-step-numbering: pass — all steps use sequential integers (Step 1 through Step N); no decimal or letter sub-steps
standards_checked.meaningful-naming: pass — all filenames are descriptive of their workflow purpose (dev, doc-gen, explain-code, file-analyze, optimize); none match the forbidden prefix patterns (enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, *-v3, improved-*, better-*, new-*)
standards_checked.english-only: pass — no Chinese or non-English characters found
standards_checked.merge-similar-scripts: pass — each script serves a distinct workflow; no overlapping functionality
standards_checked.concise-documentation: pass — no verbose prose, marketing language, or excessive examples present
standards_checked.parameterized-scripts: pass — scripts output static workflow template data; no run-variable or environment-specific values require parameterization
standards_checked.dangling-references: pass — only import is stdlib json; no internal script cross-references
